content: We can frame Self-Refine’s iterative no-supervision output refinement framework as a base structural skeleton that can be infused with multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to create a hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of no extra training or labeled data while fixing its limitation of getting stuck in local optimization traps from single-perspective feedback. This hybrid system would let an LLM bootstrap its own output quality without any external human or supervised input, by first generating an initial output, then pulling conflicting critical perspectives from its own parameter space to evaluate the output across multiple inconsistent frames, before iteratively refining it.
